title: Detection of five bacterial pathogens of rice by multiplex PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"15gh0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ae3k","text":"This multiplex PCR protocol is used for the detection of five bacterial pathogens of rice : Pseudomonas fuscovaginae, Burkholderia glumae and gladioli, Pantoea spp, Xanthomonas oryzae, Sphingomonas spp.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":91,"length":111}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"34r76","text":"- Wear clean gloves ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aengs","text":"- Clean and disinfect the PCR cabinet ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"h10e","text":"- Defreeze DNA samples and reagents ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5jq89","text":"- Gently mix the DNA samples and the 5X Hot Firepol master mix ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ulq1","text":"- Vortex the  (NH4)2SO4 and the oligonucleotides ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dd4m8","text":"- Spin down the DNA sample and all reagents and keep them on ice ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f2coc","text":"- Mark  the 1.5 ml  \"Mix\" tube   and the 0.2 ml tubes for the PCR  . Keep them on ice ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: The amounts described in this protocol are for one sample. Calculate the quantity you need based on your number of sample. Always prepare 10 % more mix.
Prepare a 2% agarose gel. Load 10µl PCR product and perform the electrophoresis at 100 Volts for 90min
Observe the gel under UV light after a ethidium bromide bath
creator: Martine Bangratz
